triggered_by.latest_user_review: The user reviewed lim8 and rejected it because abrupt translation/rotation pose jumps are still visible.
triggered_by.process_error_to_prevent: The prior loop treated lim8 as pending human review and repeated the previous task instead of honoring the latest user rejection and root-cause request.

objective: Stop local limiter tuning, identify the first-principles root cause of the remaining pose jumps, search local/internal/public EIS references for an existing principled solution, and only then choose the next implementation direction or produce an internal-AI prompt.

accepted_dependency.regular05_viewport_decision: safe103_crop98 was accepted for Regular05 because zooming and brief narrow black edge were removed.
accepted_dependency.five_clip_extension: All five Regular clips ran through the accepted EGLImage FIFO consumer with rc=0, fallback=0, and frame-index mismatch=0.
accepted_dependency.current_remaining_failure: Human review sees abrupt small translation/rotation pose changes, not zooming or scale breathing.

fresh_prompt_guard: The latest user prompt and latest human visual review override any stale active contract or pending review state.
If a candidate is pending in JSON/docs but the user has already reviewed and rejected it, first update the control plane to rejected before any new experiment.
Do not generate another neighboring limiter, smoothing radius, or scale/crop candidate until this knowledge-recovery contract has produced one concrete recovery idea, one risk, and one verifier.
Manual visual veto outranks SR_pose, residual_improve, black-border, handoff, and VPI timing metrics for quality acceptance.

allowed: safe103_crop98 fixed the prior zooming/brief-black-edge failure for Regular05 and was extended as a five-clip diagnostic path
R4 and lim8 are diagnostic evidence showing that post-hoc final-matrix smoothing trades off pose continuity against stabilization strength
the accepted C++ EGLImage FIFO consumer is healthy for these inputs
the remaining blocker is camera-path or matrix-planning quality, not device handoff health
forbidden: treating lim8 as pending or active candidate
trying lim6, lim7, lim10, another R radius, or a soft limiter before first-principles and knowledge search are complete
arguing from metrics against human-visible pose jumps
claiming zero-latency real-time EIS, zero-copy full chain, or VPI optical-flow acceleration

first_principles_frame.visual_symptom: The output camera pose appears to jump abruptly to a nearby translation/rotation state, like a sudden small camera attitude switch.
first_principles_frame.system_fact: The same device consumer reports rc=0, fallback=0, and frame-index mismatch=0, so the dataflow is consuming matrices correctly.
first_principles_frame.matrix_fact: After fixed scale, tx/ty/angle still contain large frame-to-frame stair steps. R4 reduces them but weakens stabilization too much; lim8 caps maxima but does not remove the visible discontinuity.
first_principles_frame.root_cause_hypothesis: The final per-frame warp matrices are being post-processed after the LP/viewport decision instead of solving a temporally continuous camera path with intent, derivative, confidence, and FOV constraints in one model.

source_search_order: Use internal summaries only as private guidance; do not copy internal original text into public docs.
Search public web/open-source for L1 optimal camera paths, OpenCV videostab LpMotionStabilizer, and MeshFlow/minimum-latency stabilization implementations.
If public/local evidence is insufficient for a scoped next implementation, generate a bounded prompt for company-internal AI.
success_criteria: R4 and lim8 are marked human-rejected/diagnostic in active control-plane files and public-facing summaries.
A root-cause note explains why final-matrix post-smoothing is likely the wrong abstraction.
At least one local/internal reference and one public/open-source reference are checked for a principled solution.
The next action is either a scoped implementation contract for intent-aware camera-path optimization or a copy-paste internal-AI prompt with clear context and boundaries.

root_cause_recovery_result.published_prefix_probe: Soft published-prefix continuity did not materially reduce Regular05 pose jumps; hard locking was infeasible on some clips.
root_cause_recovery_result.intent_reference_probe: LP intent-reference soft cost improved Regular05 trans_delta_p95 from 12.263541 to 10.762828 at weight 100, still worse than lim8/R4 and not enough for promotion.
root_cause_recovery_result.decision: Do not continue blind local limiter or LP-weight sweeps. Ask internal AI or mature implementation references for the correct bounded-delay intent-aware camera-path formulation.

stop_reasons: public and local knowledge do not identify a safe scoped implementation route
the next route requires mesh/rolling-shutter/gyro scope beyond the current pure-visual global path
internal company references are likely necessary to choose the correct industrial EIS path

pose_smooth_r4.reason: reduced pose jumps but weakened stabilization too much

pose_delta_limiter_lim8.reason: still has visible abrupt pose jumps

next_decision_policy.if_actionable_public_solution_found: Create or update a scoped quality-loop contract before implementing it.
next_decision_policy.if_only_broad_mesh_or_gyro_solution_foun: Report it as a model/scope boundary and generate an internal-AI prompt instead of starting a large rewrite.
next_decision_policy.if_internal_ai_needed: Generate the prompt in chat with context, boundaries, requested artifacts, and no copied internal source text in public docs.
